the_bar_pooled_ge3hop_f1.note: UNCHANGED from fix-1 — no re-pilot, no priced calls ($0 slice). CLS pooling was already used in the original pilot.

retrieval_recall_at_10.note: Per-passage recall@10 — unchanged from fix-1 (same CLS-space retrieval, same corpus)

all_bridges_present_at_k.description: Fraction of questions where ALL gold supporting passages appear in top-K (multi-hop-correct binary). Added Slice 5 fix-2. Computed $0 over 150 ≥3-hop answerable questions (same sample as retrieval_recall).
all_bridges_present_at_k.key_finding: passage_dense (0.52) >> fused (0.37) > fused_rerank (0.35) > bm25 (0.25) at K=10. RRF fusion dilutes the full-bridge set vs dense alone — the BM25 arm pulls in lexically-relevant but non-supporting passages that displace bridge passages. The CE reranker also slightly hurts the all-bridges metric vs fused (consistent with the recall finding that it demotes bridge passages). This motivates the graph PPR arm: can graph traversal recover the complete bridge set that RRF loses?

cls_pooling_resolution.evidence: bge-small-en-v1.5 ships 1_Pooling/config.json with pooling_mode_cls_token:true; BGE docs warn Mean causes significant performance drop
cls_pooling_resolution.harness_uses: CLS — BGEEncoder.encode() takes x[0] at line ~341 of m1_baseline.py
cls_pooling_resolution.documented_in: m1_baseline.py module docstring (CLS pooling section added fix-2)
cls_pooling_resolution.slice_15_prerequisite: PPR arm MUST embed in the same CLS space (inject BGEEncoder or configure engine with Pooling::Cls)

engine_mean_default_bug.impact: Stored engine vectors are in Mean space; harness/BAR vectors are in CLS space — different embedding geometries
engine_mean_default_bug.fix_requires: re-embed pass + embedder-identity change + eu7 re-measurement (see ADR-0.8.0-embedder-identity-change-workflow.md)
